title: Why Social Situations Feel Hard
description: Anticipation, judgment, and small moments of social pressure.
youtube_title: The Psychology of Social Anxiety (Before You Even Arrive)

clips.narration: Nothing has happened yet. Your body already acts like it has.
You check the clock. You rehearse what you will say.
A small closed space. Every second feels watched.
You step forward, then pull back. Approach and retreat.
A simple conversation carries hidden stakes.
Eye contact becomes evidence. Every gesture feels loud.
Even people who care can feel like an audience.
Sometimes you stop before the door because showing up feels risky.
Being seen while learning can feel like being tested as a person.
Social anxiety is often fear of judgment, not fear of people. Arrive anyway. One honest hello is enough.
